perturbation.original_text: 22. WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL. EACH OF THE PARTIES HERETO HEREBY IRREVOCABLY WAIVES ANY AND ALL RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY LEGAL PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT.
perturbation.changed_text:
15. The furnishing of Confidential Information pursuant to this Agreement does not obligate either party to enter into any further agreement or negotiation with the other or to refrain from entering into any agreement or negotiation with another party with respect to a Proposed Transaction or otherwise.

22. WAIVER OF JURY TRIAL. EACH OF THE PARTIES HERETO HEREBY IRREVOCABLY WAIVES ANY AND ALL RIGHT TO TRIAL BY JURY IN ANY LEGAL PROCEEDING ARISING OUT OF OR RELATED TO THIS AGREEMENT. This waiver applies only to disputes related to marketing strategies.
perturbation.explanation: The jury trial waiver is critical, and its enforceability depends on its clarity and conspicuousness. By appending 'This waiver applies only to disputes related to marketing strategies,' the scope of the waiver is improperly limited, potentially nullifying the waiver for other disputes arising from the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_law: 7th Amendment to the U.S. Constitution and Delaware law regarding contract interpretation
perturbation.law_explanation: The 7th Amendment guarantees the right to a jury trial in civil cases. While this right can be waived, Delaware law requires that such waivers are knowing, voluntary, and intelligent. By adding a limitation that restricts the jury waiver to only apply to disputes related to marketing strategies, it creates ambiguity about whether the parties truly intended to waive their right to a jury trial for all disputes arising from the agreement as stated in the original contract.

Contracts Subchapter II. Statute of Frauds and Perjuries Â§ 2711. Sale of goods; possession; rights of vendorâs creditors. (a) No sale, whether with or without bill of sale, of any goods or chattels, within this State, shall be good in law (except as against the vendor), or shall change or alter the property in such goods or chattels, unless a valuable consideration for the same is paid, or in good faith secured to be paid, and unless the goods and chattels sold are actually delivered into the possession of the vendee, as soon as it is convenient after the making of such sale. (b) If the goods and chattels, so sold, afterwards come into and continue in the possession of the vendor, that vendor shall be liable to the demands of all creditors. All promises and assumptions, whereby any person undertakes to answer, or pay, for the default, debt, or miscarriage, of another, any sum under $5.00, being proved by the oath or affirmation of the persons to whom such promise and assumption are made, are good and available in law to charge the party making such promise or assumption. perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces a limitation to the jury trial waiver, restricting it to disputes related to marketing strategies. This creates ambiguity regarding the waiver's applicability to other disputes arising from the agreement. The Law Explanation states that Delaware law requires jury trial waivers to be knowing, voluntary, and intelligent. By limiting the scope of the waiver, it raises concerns about whether the parties truly intended to waive their rights for all disputes, potentially undermining the waiver's enforceability beyond marketing-related disputes. Snippet 1 references Delaware law (Title 6, Chapter 27), although it focuses on other aspects of contract law. Although the snippet does not directly address jury waivers, the Law Explanation explicitly mentions Delaware law, and the changed text introduces a limitation that could render the waiver unenforceable. Therefore, the change constitutes a structural flaw that creates a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 19. Each of the parties hereto expressly agree that they will not, and will not permit any of its Representatives to directly or indirectly, hire an employee of the other party, or solicit or induce any employee of the other party to leave such employee’s position with the other party, for a period of one year. However, this paragraph shall not prohibit the Potential Acquiror or any of its affiliates from soliciting or hiring an employee who responds to a general solicitation of employment.
perturbation.changed_text:
14. This Agreement will terminate on the first anniversary of the date hereof.

19. Each of the parties hereto expressly agree that they will not, and will not permit any of its Representatives to directly or indirectly, hire an employee of the other party, or solicit or induce any employee of the other party to leave such employee’s position with the other party, for a period of one year. However, this paragraph shall not prohibit the Potential Acquiror or any of its affiliates from soliciting or hiring an employee who responds to a general solicitation of employment, unless that employee is of a certain race or gender.
perturbation.explanation: Adding discriminatory language to the non-solicitation clause renders it unenforceable and exposes the company to legal liability. This directly contradicts anti-discrimination employment laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
perturbation.law_explanation: The added phrase, "unless that employee is of a certain race or gender," explicitly introduces discriminatory criteria into the non-solicitation clause. This directly violates Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964, which prohibits employment practices that discriminate based on race, color, religion, sex, or national origin.

L. 88-352), as amended. Title VII is codified at 42 U.S.C. 2000e and in subsequent sections. Title VII amendments include those introduced by the Civil Rights Act of 1991 (CRA) and the Lilly Ledbetter Fair Pay Act of 2009 . Editor’s notes appear in italics and include cross-references to the codified version of Title VII. Title VII prohibits employment discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex and national origin. An Act To enforce the constitutional right to vote, to confer jurisdiction upon the district courts of the United States to provide injunctive relief against discrimination in public accommodations, to authorize the attorney General to institute suits to protect constitutional rights in public facilities and public education, to extend the Commission on Civil Rights, to prevent discrimination in federally assisted programs, to establish a Commission on Equal Employment Opportunity, and for other purposes. Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the United States of America in Congress assembled, That this Act may be cited as the "Civil Rights Act of 1964". * * * DEFINITIONS SEC. 2000e. [Section 701] For the purposes of this subchapter- (a) The term "person" includes one or more individuals, governments, governmental agencies, political subdivisions, labor unions, partnerships, associations, corporations, legal representatives, mutual companies, joint-­stock companies, trusts, unincorporated organizations, trustees, trustees in cases under Title 11 [originally, bankruptcy ], or receivers. (b) The term "employer" means a person engaged in an industry affecting commerce who has fifteen or more employees for each working day in each of twenty or more calendar weeks in the current or preceding calendar year, and any agent of such a person, but such term does not include (1) the United States, a corporation wholly owned by the Government of the United States, an Indian tribe, or any department or agency of the District of Columbia subject by statute to procedures of the competitive s

perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces discriminatory language ("unless that employee is of a certain race or gender") into the non-solicitation clause, which directly contradicts Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964. Scraped Law Snippet 1 states that Title VII "prohibits employment discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex and national origin." The addition of discriminatory criteria makes the clause unenforceable and exposes the company to legal liability under anti-discrimination employment laws. Therefore, a contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: 20. To the extent that any Confidential Information includes materials subject to the attorney-client privilege or similar privileges or protections, such disclosure is inadvertent and the Disclosing Party is not waiving, and shall not be deemed to have waived or diminished, its attorney work product protections, attorney-client privileges or similar protections and privileges as a result of disclosing any Confidential Information (including Confidential Information related to pending or threatened litigation) to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text:
12. The Recipient acknowledges that (i) the Disclosing Party is not obligated to provide any information pursuant to this Agreement, (ii) the Confidential Information is being given to it without liability on the part of the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives, and (iii) no representation or warranty with respect to the Confidential Information is made by the Disclosing Party or any of its Representatives.

20. To the extent that any Confidential Information includes materials subject to the attorney-client privilege or similar privileges or protections, such disclosure is inadvertent. As a result of disclosing any Confidential Information (including Confidential Information related to pending or threatened litigation) to the Receiving Party, the Disclosing Party is waiving their right to invoke attorney-client privilege.
perturbation.explanation: This section intends to protect attorney-client privilege, but the changed text claims the opposite. By stating the privilege is waived when disclosing confidential information to the receiving party, it is a direct contradiction to the purpose of attorney-client privilege.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware Rule of Evidence 502 addresses the waiver of attorney-client privilege and work product protection. The original provision aims to preserve the privilege despite inadvertent disclosure. By explicitly stating that the Disclosing Party is waiving their right to invoke attorney-client privilege, the modified text directly contradicts the intent and protections outlined in D.R.E. 502 regarding the inadvertent disclosure of privileged information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the Disclosing Party is waiving their right to invoke attorney-client privilege by disclosing confidential information to the receiving party. This directly contradicts the original text, which aimed to preserve the attorney-client privilege. The Law Explanation supports this, stating the modified text contradicts the intent and protections regarding the inadvertent disclosure of privileged information, as it explicitly waives the privilege. The absence of scraped law snippets doesn't negate the contradiction given the clear conflict between the original and changed text and the explanation provided.
